general_advisory: Go for planting vegetables immediately after rain for better utilization of rain water. Harvest the matured produce before the deterioration of quality. Staking should be given in case of banana plants to avoid lodging. Protect the mango in trees by netting from thunder storm and heavy wind. Keep the harvested paddy and non-paddy crops in safer and water proof place to avoid rainfall and high wind speed. Cover the freshly prepared vegetable nursery with polythene or with straw to avoid high temperature. Sesbania (Dhaincha) seeds @ 10 kg/acre should be sown as Green Manuring crop after summer ploughing. Apply mulches to maintain high moisture status in the soil.

advisory: Due to increased temperature and humidity there are chances of sucking pest like aphids, Jassids, thrips and whiteflies in Okra. To manage these pests at early stage of infestation spray neem-based pesticide (Neem Oil 1500 PPM @600-ml/acre by mixing it in 200-litre of water. To manage these sucking pests chemically spray Thiamethoxam 25 % WG @ 40-gram/acre or Acetamiprid 20 % SP @ 50-gram/acre.

Spray carbendazim 50WP@ 1.0g/l or Chlorothalonil 75WP@ 2.0g/l to manage anthracnose. Use fruit fly trap to manage mango fruit fly. Spray copper oxychloride 50WP @2g/l + Streptomycin @0.3g/l to manage bacterial cankar. Spraying borax @2.0g/l to manage physiological disorders.

The larvae of brinjal shoot and fruit borer burrows into the petioles and tender shoots which results in withering of terminal shoots, drooping of leaves and shedding of flower buds. To manage the pest, remove the affected terminal shoot showing bore holes and the affected fruits. To manage this insect, install 8 nos. of pheromone trap having Leucin Lure per acre. Use chemical control if there is 4% withering of terminal shoot or 14% fruits infested with borers. For chemical control spray Emamectin Benzoate 5% SG @ 80-gram/acre or Chlorantraniliprole 18.5 % SC @80-ml/acre or Spinetoram 11.7 % SC @ 200-ml /acre. There should be a gap of 7-10 days between pesticide application and harvest. Use any of the above insecticides alternately at 15-days interval.

To manage fruit fly in cucurbits, prepare a poison bait by mixing 400 g of Jaggery and 8 ml Malathion or DDVP in 4 litre of water. Keep this mixture in Plastic cups or clay pots @80 nos/acre. The fruit flies will be attracted to this and eventually get killed by falling into the poison baits. For chemical control spray Malathion 50% EC @ 2 ml/lit or Chlorantraniliprole 18.5% @ 0.25 ml/lit.
